electionCall.lede: Vote for the list that ties decisions back to students.
electionCall.title: Turn your vote into the voice of the Student Councils.

issues.title: Affordable studying
issues.text: We want study conditions to remain affordable and student perspectives to count in decision-making.

issues.title: Strong Student Councils
issues.text: Student Councils are our basis: this is where issues, motions and political feedback from degree programs emerge.

issues.title: Antifascist university
issues.text: We stand for an open university where discrimination and right-wing intimidation have no place.

issues.title: Open culture
issues.text: Student spaces, culture and political education belong in everyday university life and need reliable support.

items.title: Get informed
items.text: Learn about the FSVV, our system and your Student Council.

items.title: Request postal voting
items.text: If you cannot vote in person: request postal voting in time and vote without stress.

items.title: Vote FSVV
items.text: Cast your vote, find your polling place and remind your friends: participation lives from people taking part.

org.title: Our System
paragraphs: The Fachschaftenvollversammlung is the independent student representation at the University of Tübingen. Since the 1970s, Student Councils have brought together issues from studies, everyday university life and committees there.

Every Monday, delegates from the Student Councils meet in the Klubhaus. Decisions are discussed and made collectively and openly; representatives in the Studierendenrat and Senat are bound by these decisions.

defaultDetail.title: Choose an element in the chart
defaultDetail.text: Click a node in the chart to see how participation is passed on in our system.

Studierende.title: All students
Studierende.text: All students can bring issues to their Student Councils, join discussions and help shape student self-governance.

Fachschaften.title: Student Councils
Fachschaften.text: Up to two delegates per Student Council come to the FSVV on Mondays. Issues are carried back from the FSVV into the Student Councils and discussed openly there.

FSVV.title: FSVV
FSVV.text: The FSVV is independent, grassroots-democratic and not party-political. It brings together the perspectives of the Student Councils and adopts joint decisions.

StuRa.title: Studierendenrat
StuRa.text: Representatives in the Studierendenrat are bound by FSVV decisions and vote there as previously decided collectively.

Senat.title: Senat
Senat.text: In the Senat, too, student voices should come transparently from the Student Councils and the FSVV instead of being detached from the basis.

process.title: From motion to vote
process.stepsAria: How participation works in the FSVV
steps: A motion reaches the FSVV: from the Student Councils or from the Studierendenrat
The FSVV discusses the motion and passes it on to the Student Councils
Students discuss and decide in their Student Councils
Delegates bring the feedback back to the FSVV
Our mandate holders then vote accordingly in the Studierendenrat

faculty.stamp: From the subjects, for the subjects
faculty.title: Your Student Council is the starting point
paragraphs: Your Student Council knows everyday life in your degree program: exam chaos, overcrowded seminars, first-semester orientation, advice, Clubhaus parties and the small problems that otherwise tend to disappear. That is exactly why our system is based on the Student Councils. What happens in the departments is carried into the FSVV and developed collectively.
In turn, the FSVV gives the Student Councils, which work locally for good study conditions, the voice they need in university politics.

vote.text: All enrolled students at the University of Tübingen can vote for student representatives.

mail.summary: How do I request postal voting?
mail.text: You can request postal voting documents online. According to the university, this is possible until June 16, 2026, but only from the university network. If you are not using university Wi-Fi, you need to use eduVPN.

location.summary: Where can I vote on election days?
location.intro: Polling places are open on June 23 and 24, 2026, from 9:00 to 17:00. Your polling place depends on your faculty or degree program:
locations: Clubhaus: Protestant Theology, Catholic Theology, Law, Economics and Social Sciences, Center for Islamic Theology, Dentistry, Psychology and selected geosciences.
Brecht-Bau, entrance hall: Faculty of Humanities and Leibniz Kolleg.
Lecture Hall Center Morgenstelle, foyer: Biology, Chemistry, Computer Science, Mathematics, Pharmacy, Biochemistry, Physics and the other geosciences.
CRONA, entrance hall in front of lecture hall 210: Faculty of Medicine except Dentistry.
location.note: No guarantee for these details. You can find more precise information here:

work.summary: What does the FSVV actually do?
work.text: The FSVV is the association of Student Councils. Issues are discussed openly there, carried back into the Student Councils and then decided collectively.

beaver.summary: Who is inside the beaver costume?
beaver.text: That remains a small campaign secret. What is certain: the beaver is not running for office itself; it simply draws attention to the election and appears whenever university politics needs it. Everything else is legend.

candidates.summary: Who exactly am I voting for?
candidates.intro: With the FSVV, the individual person matters less than the shared mandate. Our representatives are bound by the decisions of the Fachschaftenvollversammlung. Through this imperative mandate, as many students from the Student Councils as possible should be involved in decision-making instead of individual elected representatives voting independently.
